channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global Geopolitical News Headlines - Tier 1 Sources

item.title: 🚀 Iran Replenishing Missile Launchers Faster Than Before War, Guards Commander Says
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force commander said the country now updates and replenishes its missile and drone launchers at a higher speed than before the war with the US and Israel. The statement signals Iran's continued military capacity despite weeks of strikes.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Decries Escalation in Ukraine War, Calls for Weapons to Fall Silent
item.description: Pope Leo on Sunday decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The appeal comes amid Russia's recent mass missile attacks that killed at least 17 civilians.

item.title: 🛡️ Ukraine Pushes Europe to Build Ballistic Missile Defense System
item.description: President Zelenskiy said Europe must have its own defense system against ballistic weapons, with Ukraine already holding talks with several countries on its creation. The push comes as ballistic missile defense remains one of Ukraine's biggest challenges in the war with Russia.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian Former President Set for Landslide Win in Bulgaria Election
item.description: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set for a runaway victory in Bulgaria's election and may even secure a parliamentary majority, exit polls showed. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and alter the EU member's foreign policy orientation.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇬🇷 France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact for Five Years Amid Iran War
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Greece next week to renew a security pact between the two countries for another five years, a Greek government official said. The renewal comes against the backdrop of the war in iran and shifting European security dynamics.

item.title: ⛪ Israeli Soldier Filmed Smashing Jesus Statue in Lebanon Sparks Outrage
item.description: An image showing an Israeli soldier using a jackhammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon went viral, sparking outrage including among US conservatives. The incident in the Maronite Christian village of Debel drew condemnation from figures like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz.

item.title: 🚢 Iran Vows 'Swift Response' After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Hormuz
item.description: Iran's military vowed retaliation after US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the Navy 'blew a hole in the engineroom' of the vessel Touska. Meanwhile, Brent crude surged 7% to $96.85 as markets reacted.

item.title: 🤖 Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Half-Marathon by 6 Minutes
item.description: A humanoid robot named Lightning completed a half-marathon in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds—beating the human world record by over 6 minutes. The race attracted 105 robot competitors, showcasing China's rapid advances in humanoid robotics.

item.title: ⚖️ Iranian National Charged in US With Trafficking Arms to Sudan for Tehran
item.description: US federal prosecutors charged Iranian national Shamim Mafi with brokering the sale of drones, bombs, and millions of rounds of ammunition manufactured by Iran and sold to Sudan. Mafi faces up to 20 years in prison if convicted.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine's Long-Range Strikes Inflicted $2.3 Billion in Russian Oil Losses in March
item.description: Ukrainian estimates show long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure caused at least $2.3 billion in lost oil revenue for Russia in March alone. The attacks disabled about 40% of Russia's oil export capacity.

item.title: 📉 Gulf Equities Mixed as Hormuz Uncertainty Caps Ceasefire Optimism
item.description: Gulf equities ended mixed on Sunday after President Trump said Iran had committed a serious violation of the ceasefire, renewing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the state of US-Iran talks despite his insistence that a peace deal would happen.

item.title: 💵 Dollar Rebounds as Middle East Tensions Reignite, Hormuz Closed
item.description: The dollar headed for its highest level in a week at the start of Asian trading as renewed tensions in the Middle East sent investors scurrying for safe havens. Iran's reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz sparked fresh risk aversion.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Jumps, Stock Futures Slip as Iran Tensions Unsettle Markets
item.description: Oil prices jumped, the US dollar rose and stock futures fell as investors dealt with conflicting messages about the Iran war and news that the Strait of Hormuz was closed again. The renewed uncertainty overshadowed ceasefire hopes.

item.title: 🇰🇵 Kim Jong Un Oversees Test of New Cluster Warhead Missile as North Korea Accelerates Weapons Program
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw test launches of upgraded short-range ballistic missiles including the Hwasong-11 Ra equipped with a cluster warhead. The test demonstrates Pyongyang's continued weapons development despite international pressure.

item.title: 📉 IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Outlook, Warns of Recession Risk From Iran War
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has trimmed its global growth forecast for 2026, warning that a prolonged Iran war could significantly worsen the outlook if energy prices surge further.

item.title: ⚖️ Michigan Attorney General Rejects Trump Administration Ballot Request in Election Push
item.description: Michigan's attorney general is rejecting an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain ballots and other voting materials, pushing back against what critics call a broader administration effort to challenge election administration.

item.title: 📊 Trump Approval Rating Hits Record Low in NBC News Poll as Iran War Drags On
item.description: A new NBC News poll shows the war in Iran is dragging down President Trump's approval rating, with just 37% of American adults approving of his performance. The poll reflects growing public concern about the prolonged conflict.

item.title: ⛽ Most Voters Blame Trump for Rising Gas Prices, Quinnipiac Poll Shows
item.description: Most registered voters blame President Trump for rising gas prices, according to a new Quinnipiac University national poll. The survey highlights the political cost of the Iran war's impact on energy prices ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Rebound 7% as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
item.description: Oil prices rebounded more than 7% on Monday after tumbling more than 9% on Friday as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, reversing a brief reopening. Brent crude futures jumped $6.56 to $96.94 a barrel as the vital waterway remains blocked.

item.title: 🚗 EV Sales Soar in Europe as Iran War Drives Petrol Prices to Record Highs
item.description: Sales of fully electric cars in Europe's main auto markets jumped by almost a third in the first quarter of 2026, as drivers sought alternatives to combustion engines after the Iran war caused the highest spike in petrol prices in years.

item.title: ⛽ Bangladesh Raises Fuel Prices 10-15% as Iran War Drives Up Global Costs
item.description: Bangladesh raised retail fuel prices by 10% to 15%, citing a sharp surge in global crude oil prices and tightening supplies caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🛰️ Two Foreigners Arrested in Iran for Importing Starlink Technology
item.description: Iranian authorities arrested four individuals, including two foreign nationals, on charges of being part of a 'U.S.-Israel-linked espionage network' for importing Starlink satellite internet technology.

item.title: ⛽ US Energy Chief Warns Gas Prices Could Stay Above $3 Until Next Year
item.description: The US Energy Secretary warned that gasoline prices could remain above $3 per gallon through next year, with the average price at $4.05 per gallon compared to $3.16 a year ago.

item.title: 🏦 US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
item.description: IMF spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies and defense alliances, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow.
